and you get to associate yourself with them. So this business is about creating a reputation that you become known for. The page, first thing to do is decide what it's going to be. All about decision. Who do you want to be? You want to be the wise old grizzled veteran of your industry? Do you want to be you know the arrogant young punk who annoys everybody? Do you want to be obnoxious? Do you want to be warm and fuzzy? Do you want to be what do you want to be? You get to decide because you're making it up from scratch. And of course you ought to pick based on what's going to be effective for you. Now you can't totally be something you aren't. So you got to pick characteristics you know that you can work with. But effectiveness is what it's all about. So there's decisions to be made about what you are going to set out to make yourself known as and known for. You're known for competencies. You're known as a type of person. So you may be known as somebody who's very difficult to work with. And you're known for your ability to take dental practices and magically ten times them in size overnight. You may be known as an enormously compassionate empathetic father figure. You would be known for some competency. You have to pick what those things are and build your shortlist and then make sure that you do everything strategically to reinforce the shortlist that you've decided on. So for a number of years I did work for a guy by the name he doesn't do much anymore. His name is Dick Sutfin, S-U-T-P-H-E-N. And Dick's were studying for a variety of reasons. One is he was a he was a metaphysical community star for a number of years and a past lives person. Big seminar once a year in Sedona where 500 people come and they all lay on the floor and roll around and flop and vomit and stuff and have simultaneous past lives experiences and return to Atlantis and they were all Napoleon. Nobody by the way in their past in their past life was ever like a beggar. They were all emperors and emperors and knights of the round table and queen of Atlantis and so forth. But anyway so like Dick had a bestselling book by the way on New York Times list for a number of years called Past Lives Future Loves all about past life stuff which our joke was that the title should have been Past Lives Future Alimony based upon the number of times that Dick was married. But anyway so so this so this is Dick's positioning. So Dick for example had to have long hair. Anything else would have been unacceptable for who he was and and he couldn't come in the room in a three-piece pinstripe suit and a tie that would not be acceptable for who he was. Now who Dick actually was was an advertising copywriter and executive from New York who got tired of being and had three piece pinstripes suits and ties and that's pretty much all he ever wore while he was in New York and short hair and but but he got tired of being an advertising copywriter and went in search of a niche and a market and so forth and he transformed himself into this larger than life character and but he had to make sure everything was congruent with the short list of things that he decided that he was going to be known as and known for. Hence the future alimony problem because they kept marrying him thinking they were getting that thing they saw up on stage and you know who sat up on the roof in the rain and wrote poetry and then after about three months of discovering that you know he was us. Enormous disillusionment set in then off we go to divorce court but so you choose this thing and then you build it. Now if you don't consciously choose and you don't consciously and strategically build it by the way you wind up with a reputation anyway.
